evals.prompt: Turn the confirmed session summary in evals/files/repo-hotfix-release.md into a reusable skill. This is a benchmark run with no live user, so treat the file contents as already-confirmed answers and do not ask follow-up questions. Save the generated skill to outputs/SKILL.md and write outputs/output.md describing the recommended save path, invocation command, and any assumptions.
evals.expected_output: A repo-scoped ship-hotfix skill with explicit allowed-tools, step success criteria, and a human checkpoint before merge/deploy.
expectations: Frontmatter includes name, description, and allowed-tools entries inferred from the session
The generated skill is inline or user-interactive rather than forked for the hotfix workflow
Every major step includes explicit success criteria
The merge or deploy step includes a human checkpoint
Frontmatter includes argument metadata for pr_number and target_environment

evals.prompt: Turn the confirmed session summary in evals/files/personal-docs-to-sop.md into a reusable skill. This is a benchmark run with no live user, so treat the file contents as already-confirmed answers and do not ask follow-up questions. Save the generated skill to outputs/SKILL.md and write outputs/output.md describing the recommended save path, invocation command, and any assumptions.
evals.expected_output: A personal doc-to-sop skill with arguments, argument hint, minimal non-shell tools, and clear invocation guidance.
expectations: Frontmatter includes arguments and argument-hint for the confirmed inputs
Frontmatter includes a when_to_use trigger description tailored to doc-to-sop requests
Every major step includes explicit success criteria
allowed-tools stays within document-oriented tools and does not add Bash
The workflow explicitly avoids inventing shell commands or package installation steps unless the source requires them

This is a benchmark run with no live user, so treat the file contents as already-confirmed answers and do not ask follow-up questions. evals.expected_output: A repo-scoped forked benchmark-triage skill that preserves the task-agent execution pattern and documents self-contained execution.

allowed-tools includes the benchmark-analysis core tool mix from the session summary
